items.title: Samsung’s regularly $1,000 500Hz OLED Odyssey gaming monitor is down to $650 today ($350 off)
items.summary:
As part of its updated Samsung monitor sale, Woot is offering the brand’s 27-inch Odyssey G6 (G60SF) 500Hz OLED gaming monitor for $649.99 Prime shipped (a $6 delivery fee will apply otherwise). This particular model hit the scene last year as the ‘world’s first 500Hz OLED’ gaming display carrying a $1,000 price tag. While it’s still fetching that price directly at Samsung, you’ll typically find it listed for $920 at Amazon. Today’s offer at Woot, however, takes $350 off its usual going rate, marking one of the lowest prices we have tracked for it to date. This is the lowest price we’ve seen for this unit aside from a brief $610 drop at Woot that very few people were able to grab, so be sure to lock this price in after getting more details below.

items.title: Alienware’s value-packed 34-inch WQHD 180Hz curved gaming monitor is down to $290 today (2026 low)
items.summary:
We just spotted a deal at Amazon that drops Alienware’s 34-inch WQHD 180Hz curved gaming monitor down to $289.99 shipped. This already affordable, value-packed model typically goes for $350, so you’re looking at a $60 discount on it today. While it’s not an all-time low by a huge margin, this is still the steepest price drop we’ve tracked for this model so far this year and one of the lowest prices we’ve seen overall. Only once before today have we seen this display go for less, and it’s now within just $20 of that price. It’s a great budget option to consider if you’re in the market to buy an ultrawide monitor, and you can learn more about it in detail below.

items.title: This tiny 45W power bank with built-in USB-C cable is among the smallest 20,000mAh models we’ve seen, now down to just $30
items.summary:
Baseus PicoGo AC22 Mini 20,000mAh 45W power bank with built-in USB-C cable

Buy for $30 (Reg. $50)

Apply BASEUSAC22B during checkout.

What we love

This compact Baseus power bank packs a massive 20,000mAh battery inside, which is enough to top up your phone multiple times on the go. Its tiny shell makes it super easy to carry around, and the built-in USB-C cable here doubles as a handy lanyard cable. It can deliver up to 45W of power to your device via the built-in cord, and it also has an additional Type-C port in case you need to charge another device.

This is one of the latest Razer headsets that hit the scene toward the end of last year with a $180 price tag. Today’s deal delivers a 25% markdown to shave $45 of its usual going rate. We’ve seen the Mercury White and Quartz Pink variants of this headset go for less, but this is the lowest price we have seen the black model fetch to date. They’re all down to the $135 mark today, though, so you can pick the one you like after getting more details below.
